SUMMARY:

--- IMAGES ---

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://image.slidesharecdn.com/mrinmoyspresentation-191207172634/75/Conventional-and-Modern-Breeding-for-Disease-Resistant-Program-in-Plant-13-2048.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A presentation slide highlights the advantages of breeding for disease resistance in agriculture, emphasizing benefits such as cost-effective disease control, environmental reduction of fungicide use, and safeguarding against the accidental release of vulnerable varieties.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://image4.slideserve.com/9141959/plant-breeding-for-disease-resistance-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates the importance of disease resistance breeding in agriculture, emphasizing breeding cultivars to combat fungi, viruses, and bacteria in tropical climates to prevent crop loss and increase food production.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://i.ytimg.com/vi/YLKwL2ajlLQ/maxresdefault.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates the concept of disease resistance breeding in agriculture, showing how a tobacco mosaic virus affects plants by causing black, yellow, and green leaf symptoms.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://i.ytimg.com/vi/_H2DB5YHsFI/maxresdefault.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A large, well-maintained agricultural field with rows of lush green lettuce and illustrations of maize and a stem borer indicating plant breeding efforts for insect pest resistance.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image.slideserve.com/1097877/slide14-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A presentation slide highlighting the importance of disease resistance breeding in agriculture, emphasizing its advantages such as eliminating chemical control and requiring no post-cultivar action, along with its disadvantages like potential resistance failure and the need for expertise in pathogen virulence.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Disease resistance breeding is crucial for developing resilient crop varieties, ensuring food security while minimizing reliance on chemical pesticides and enhancing sustainable agricultural practices.

enriched_points.point_title: Integration of traditional and modern techniques
enriched_points.expanded_content: Combining traditional plant breeding with advanced molecular techniques enhances the efficiency of developing disease-resistant cultivars, allowing for faster adaptation to emerging threats in agriculture.

Advancements in genetic engineering and biotechnology will drive the next generation of disease-resistant crops, supporting sustainable agricultural systems while addressing climate change and increasing global food demands.
